label: Batch Packing (2D)
blurb: packing work into a fixed-capacity resource in as few batches as possible — keep the resource full for throughput without overflowing its memory (batching, scheduling, parallelism, GPU/vLLM serving, bin-packing).
classify: packing many items into a fixed-capacity resource across batches to maximize throughput without overflowing memory (batching, scheduling, pipelining, parallelism, GPU serving / vLLM, bin-packing). Choose for throughput / utilization / 'why batch' concepts.
levelFormat: node.level = {capacity:int, budget:int (max batches for a clean win), requests:[{id:string,size:int ≤ capacity}]}, with sum(sizes) ≤ capacity×budget so it is solvable. Theme has vocab{resource,item,batch,capacityLabel,unit,runWord,hint} + visual{accent,actorIcon}; each node entry {title,hook,winText,failText?} with failText keys "underutilize" and "overcommit". Node entries MAY also optionally carry learn:{frame?,reveal:{concept?,body,inTheWild?},insights?} (shell-sequenced frame→play→reveal beats, optional).
drillLevelFormat: For batch-packing: node.level = {capacity:int, budget:int, requests:[{id,size ≤ capacity}]} with sum(sizes) ≤ capacity×budget. failText keys are "underutilize" and "overcommit".
